[[User:Addbot|Bot:]] Migrating 11 interwiki links, now provided by [[Wikipedia:Wikidata|Wikidata]] on [[d:q8235744]]
The Air Purifier uses a needlepoint ionizer to drop dust, smoke and other harmful particles from the air we breathe.
revision.text: The process includes: 1) The [http://airpurifiersdirectly.com/ Air Purifier] uses a needlepoint ionizer to drop dust, smoke and other harmful particles from the air we breathe. In our homes and offices there is a lack of negative ions and an abundance of positive ions. The buildings we spend most of our time in block out the natural occurring negative ions. The [http://airpurifiersdirectly.com/ Air Purifier] recreates the natural balance of positive to negative ions to significantly reduce airborne particulate and 2) The [http://airpurifiersdirectly.com/ Air Purifier] uses electricity to split oxygen O2 molecules into single o1 atoms, which attach loosely to other O2 molecules to become O3 (activated oxygen). [http://airpurifiersdirectly.com/ Air Purifiers]

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contribs/Albertbond12|Albertbond12]] ([[User talk:Albertbond12|talk]]) to last version by OlEnglish
Ultimate 100 British hotels
revision.text:
Last September, we published our first edition of The Sunday Times Ultimate 100 British Hotels. We know from your feedback that it hit the spot, with thousands of you using the guide to plan your breaks. But things change fast in the hotel world: standards soar and dive, places open and close, innovations arise and tastes move on. You needed an update. Here it is. This second edition is completely newly researched. Our writers visited and revisited hundreds of hotels around the country. They were looking for the essentials, of course: good service, comfort, value. But on their own, those weren’t enough to merit inclusion. The brief was to find hotels that offer something less tangible, but just as important: character, confidence and, above all, warmth. We wanted to find hotels you could love.
Source [http://www.str-ly.com/vb/t184137 str-ly.com]

revision.text:
{{Wikipedia category|hidden=yes}} {{polluted category}} {{backlog|5000}} {{shortcut|CAT:ALLDL}}
== '''Advancements, Benefits, Limitations, and Impact on the Healthcare Industry:''' == '''Robot-assisted surgery''' has revolutionized the field of '''healthcare''', combining the precision of robotics with the expertise of human surgeons.
== Introduction: == With advancements in technology, '''[[Robotics|robotic surgery]]''' systems have become increasingly prevalent in hospitals worldwide. This article explores the remarkable progress in robot-assisted surgery, examining its benefits, limitations, and the profound impact it has on the healthcare industry
=== Advancements in Robotic Surgery : === Over the years, robotic surgery systems have evolved significantly, enhancing surgical precision and expanding the scope of procedures that can be performed robotically. One of the key advancements is the use of robotic arms equipped with instruments that offer greater dexterity and maneuverability. Surgeons can control these robotic arms with precision, enabling precise movements within the patient's body.
Another major breakthrough is the incorporation of 3D imaging and high-definition visualization systems. These technologies provide surgeons with a detailed view of the surgical site, enhancing their ability to identify anatomical structures and perform complex procedures with enhanced accuracy.
=== Benefits of Robot-Assisted Surgery : === '''Robot-'''assisted surgery brings numerous benefits to both surgeons and patients. One significant advantage is the enhanced precision and control offered by robotic systems. The robotic arms can filter out any hand tremors and allow for precise movements, reducing the risk of human error and improving surgical outcomes. This precision is particularly crucial in delicate procedures such as neurosurgery and microsurgery.
Additionally, robot-assisted surgery often leads to smaller incisions and less scarring, resulting in faster recovery times and reduced post-operative pain for patients. The minimally invasive nature of robotic surgery can also minimize blood loss, '''robo rock''' decrease the risk of infection, and shorten hospital stays, ultimately reducing healthcare costs.
Furthermore, robot-assisted surgery enables remote surgery, where a surgeon can operate on a patient located in a different geographical location. This capability has the potential to extend surgical expertise to underserved areas, providing access to specialized surgical care that was previously unavailable.
=== Limitations and Challenges: === While robot-assisted surgery offers significant advantages, it is not without limitations and challenges. One primary concern is the high cost associated with acquiring and maintaining robotic systems. The initial investment, ongoing maintenance, and specialized training for surgeons can be financially burdensome for healthcare institutions.
Another limitation lies in the steep learning curve for surgeons transitioning from traditional to robotic surgery. Mastering the use of robotic systems and becoming proficient in their operation requires substantial training and practice.
Additionally, the reliance on technology introduces the risk of technical malfunctions or system errors during surgery. Surgeons must be prepared to swiftly respond to such situations and possess the necessary skills to convert to traditional surgical methods if required.
=== Impact on the Healthcare Industry : === The impact of robot-assisted surgery on the healthcare industry is profound. It has opened up new avenues for complex procedures and improved patient outcomes. The technology's precision and minimal invasiveness have resulted in reduced complication rates, shorter hospital stays, and quicker recoveries.
Furthermore, the integration of robotics in surgical procedures attracts patients seeking the latest advancements in healthcare, positively impacting the reputation and competitiveness of hospitals and healthcare institutions.
Moreover, the continuous development of robotic surgery systems drives research and innovation in the medical field, leading to further advancements and improved surgical techniques. It also encourages interdisciplinary collaboration between robotics engineers, computer scientists, and healthcare professionals, fostering a synergistic approach to medical technology development.
=== Conclusion : === '''humanoid Robot'''-assisted surgery has transformed the healthcare industry by offering enhanced precision, improved patient outcomes, and expanded surgical possibilities. Despite the challenges and limitations, the benefits of robotic surgery are evident, leading to reduced pain, faster recovery, and increased access to specialized care. As technology continues to advance, robotic surgery systems hold immense potential for further innovation and the evolution of surgical techniques. With ongoing research and continuous refinement of these systems, the future of robot-assisted surgery promises to revolutionize healthcare and improve the quality of patient care.{{center|{{Random page in category}}}} {{CollapsibleLargeCategoryTOC}}
[[Category:Articles with dead external links| ]] [[Category:Monthly clean-up category (Articles with dead external links) counter]]

{{Wikipedia category|hidden=yes}} https://deilyblogs0x.blogspot.com/2023/05/robot-assisted-surgery-advancements.html<nowiki/>{{polluted category}} {{backlog|5000}} {{shortcut|CAT:ALLDL}}
== '''Advancements, Benefits, Limitations, and Impact on the Healthcare Industry:''' == '''Robot-assisted surgery''' has revolutionized the field of '''healthcare''', combining the precision of robotics with the expertise of human surgeons.
== Introduction: == With advancements in technology, '''[[Robotics|robotic surgery]]''' systems have become increasingly prevalent in hospitals worldwide. This article explores the remarkable progress in robot-assisted surgery, examining its benefits, limitations, and the profound impact it has on the healthcare industry
=== Advancements in Robotic Surgery : === Over the years, robotic surgery systems have evolved significantly, enhancing surgical precision and expanding the scope of procedures that can be performed robotically. One of the key advancements is the use of robotic arms equipped with instruments that offer greater dexterity and maneuverability. Surgeons can control these robotic arms with precision, enabling precise movements within the patient's body.
Another major breakthrough is the incorporation of 3D imaging and high-definition visualization systems. These technologies provide surgeons with a detailed view of the surgical site, enhancing their ability to identify anatomical structures and perform complex procedures with enhanced accuracy.
=== Benefits of Robot-Assisted Surgery : === '''Robot-'''assisted surgery brings numerous benefits to both surgeons and patients. One significant advantage is the enhanced precision and control offered by robotic systems. The robotic arms can filter out any hand tremors and allow for precise movements, reducing the risk of human error and improving surgical outcomes. This precision is particularly crucial in delicate procedures such as neurosurgery and microsurgery.
Additionally, robot-assisted surgery often leads to smaller incisions and less scarring, resulting in faster recovery times and reduced post-operative pain for patients. The minimally invasive nature of robotic surgery can also minimize blood loss, '''robo rock''' decrease the risk of infection, and shorten hospital stays, ultimately reducing healthcare costs.
Furthermore, robot-assisted surgery enables remote surgery, where a surgeon can operate on a patient located in a different geographical location. This capability has the potential to extend surgical expertise to underserved areas, providing access to specialized surgical care that was previously unavailable.
=== Limitations and Challenges: === While robot-assisted surgery offers significant advantages, it is not without limitations and challenges. One primary concern is the high cost associated with acquiring and maintaining robotic systems. The initial investment, ongoing maintenance, and specialized training for surgeons can be financially burdensome for healthcare institutions.
Another limitation lies in the steep learning curve for surgeons transitioning from traditional to robotic surgery. Mastering the use of robotic systems and becoming proficient in their operation requires substantial training and practice.
Additionally, the reliance on technology introduces the risk of technical malfunctions or system errors during surgery. Surgeons must be prepared to swiftly respond to such situations and possess the necessary skills to convert to traditional surgical methods if required.
=== Impact on the Healthcare Industry : === The impact of robot-assisted surgery on the healthcare industry is profound. It has opened up new avenues for complex procedures and improved patient outcomes. The technology's precision and minimal invasiveness have resulted in reduced complication rates, shorter hospital stays, and quicker recoveries.
Furthermore, the integration of robotics in surgical procedures attracts patients seeking the latest advancements in healthcare, positively impacting the reputation and competitiveness of hospitals and healthcare institutions.
Moreover, the continuous development of robotic surgery systems drives research and innovation in the medical field, leading to further advancements and improved surgical techniques. It also encourages interdisciplinary collaboration between robotics engineers, computer scientists, and healthcare professionals, fostering a synergistic approach to medical technology development.
=== Conclusion : === '''humanoid Robot'''-assisted surgery has transformed the healthcare industry by offering enhanced precision, improved patient outcomes, and expanded surgical possibilities. Despite the challenges and limitations, the benefits of robotic surgery are evident, leading to reduced pain, faster recovery, and increased access to specialized care. As technology continues to advance, robotic surgery systems hold immense potential for further innovation and the evolution of surgical techniques. With ongoing research and continuous refinement of these systems, the future of robot-assisted surgery promises to revolutionize healthcare and improve the quality of patient care.{{center|{{Random page in category}}}} {{CollapsibleLargeCategoryTOC}}
[[Category:Articles with dead external links| ]] [[Category:Monthly clean-up category (Articles with dead external links) counter]]

181 revisions imported from [[:wikipedia:Category:All_articles_with_dead_external_links]]
Copying from [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] to [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Removing from [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Copying from [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] to [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia2]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Removing from [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia2]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Copying from [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules]] to [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Removing from [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates. [[{page.name}]]
Bot: tidy & size tag (106 members)
Bot: tidy & size tag (1 members)
Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Monthly clean-up category (Articles with dead external links) counter]]
Bot: tag as Tier 0 category
Bot: confirm enwiki category & retag
Text replacement - "↵↵" to " "
Text replacement - "[[Category:confirmed enwiki categories]]" to "[[en:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[category:confirmed enwiki categories]]"
Bot: remove obsolete category Categories with 1 members
Bot: remove obsolete category Confirmed enwiki categories
Bot: tidy cats/IWs; add JA prose & tracking
Bot: prepend JA text, fix translated-page + cats
Bot: remove obsolete category Tier 1 Categories
Bot: prepend JA text, fix translated-page + cats
Bot: prepend JA text, fix translated-page & cats
Bot: prepend JA text, fix translated-page + cats
Text replacement - "{{draft categories|↵↵}}↵" to ""
Bot: move cats/inter-wikis/translated-page to bottom
Text replacement - "==Old Content==↵==" to "=="
Text replacement - "==Old Content==↵==" to "=="
Bot: tag as missing wikidata (no wikidata found via interwikis)
Bot: add wikidata links from interwikis (Q8235744)
Bot: remove [[Category:categories missing wikidata]] (has wikidata link)
Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: reorganize categories (alphabetical, deduplicated, moved to bottom)
adding dummy category to make categories work
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Text replacement - "[[Category:pages]]" to "[[Category:Categories]]"
Bot: remove [[Category:Categories]] (crud category cleanup)
Bot: remove [[Category:Original categories]] (crud category cleanup)
Bot: normalize category page structure (templates/interwikis/categories only)
